title: Use of Had
description: Master the use of 'Had' — past form of 'have'. Learn to express past possession, past experiences, and form past perfect sentences. Had is one of the most important words in English grammar for expressing the past.

subtopics.title: Had for Past Possession
subtopics.description: I had a car. She had a dog. Expressing what someone possessed in the past.

subtopics.title: Had in Simple Past
subtopics.description: He had fever. They had food. Using had as main verb in simple past.

subtopics.title: Past Perfect with Had
subtopics.description: I had eaten. She had gone. Forming past perfect tense with had + V3.

subtopics.title: Had in Various Sentence Types
subtopics.description: Affirmative, negative, and question sentences using had.

subtopics.title: Common Usage of Had
subtopics.description: Had better, had to, had a good time — common expressions.

subtopics.title: Common Errors with Had
subtopics.description: I have gone yesterday (wrong!), She had went (wrong!) — fixing mistakes.

learningObjectives: Use had to express past possession
Use had as main verb in simple past sentences
Form past perfect tense using had + past participle
Make negative sentences with had not / hadn't
Use common expressions like had better, had to
